prompt: An ultra-detailed cinematic isometric street-level view of a dense nostalgic Japanese canal-side neighborhood at blue hour, designed like a miniature lived-in urban diorama. The scene is packed with tightly clustered multi-story buildings of varied shapes and ages, with weathered concrete, faded painted walls, patched metal roofs, tiled roofs, small balconies, rooftop tanks, antennas, pipes, exposed wiring, hanging laundry, window air conditioners, satellite dishes, potted plants, vines, and warm glowing interior lights in nearly every window. At the center, place a charming teal-blue shop house with a dark blue tiled roof, rounded windows, exterior stairs, a tiny awning, and a bright retro sign reading トリナオ. Surround it with many neighboring storefronts and residences stacked irregularly at different heights, creating a layered maze of alleys, staircases, landings, bridges, and utility poles. Include exactly 2 vending machines near the lower center-right area, exactly 2 bicycles along the canal-side walkway, exactly 1 satellite dish on an upper roof near the center, exactly 1 large round wall clock mounted on a cylindrical corner building on the right, and exactly 1 canal in the foreground with dark reflective water and a curved concrete embankment. Add numerous vertical Japanese shop signs and lit billboards throughout the composition, including signs resembling 久保田, 山城商店, and other retro neighborhood business signage. The architecture should feel hand-crafted, eclectic, slightly whimsical, and deeply lived in, with dense environmental storytelling and no empty space. Lighting is soft dusk light mixed with warm amber practical lights from windows, lanterns, and signs, creating a cozy yet bustling mood. Color palette: teal, muted red, worn cream, mossy green, rust, indigo, and warm tungsten yellow. Style: hyper-detailed photoreal miniature cityscape, cinematic depth, crisp textures, subtle atmospheric haze, high dynamic range, Japanese Showa-era inspired urban nostalgia, richly layered composition, no visible people, wide landscape framing.
